steps.title: Connect action to result
steps.content: The first sentence says Mercer explores Alvarez's sculpture. The second says he participates in ekphrasis, a poetic tradition involving visual art. The transition must show that the act of exploring the sculpture is how Mercer joins that tradition.

steps.title: Test the transition choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: Adds another point, but the second sentence explains the significance of the first.
  • Choice B: Means by doing the action just described, which links Mercer's exploration to ekphrasis.
  • Choice C: Signals concession, but the sentences are not opposed.
  • Choice D: Signals direct contrast, which the passage does not create.
The answer is B.
